test_cases.name: Radiographic Testing - Weld Defect Interpretation
test_cases.situation: RT of 20-inch X65 weld shows 1.5-inch elongated indication parallel to axis, root location, darker density, well-defined linear. Wall 0.500 inches. Schedule pressure to accept.
test_cases.task: Classify defect per API 1104, determine acceptability, specify repairs, address ethics.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: INCOMPLETE PENETRATION per API 1104 Section 9.1.2. Table 4: Aggregate IP limit 1 inch per 12 inches. This 1.5 inch exceeds. REJECT per Section 9.3.3. Repair length 5.5 inches (1.5+2+2), 30-degree groove, 100% RT. Ethics: 49 CFR 192.241(c) requires compliance, schedule pressure NEVER justifies compromise.

test_cases.name: Ultrasonic Thickness - Corrosion Assessment
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Location A: 15% - monitor. Location B: 28% - scheduled repair within 1 year. Location C: 4% - routine. Corrosion rate: 4.1 mpy. Hoop stress at B = 41,667 psi > 36,000 design - reduce pressure to 864 psig per API 1160.

test_cases.name: AC/DC Interference Mitigation
test_cases.situation: Pipeline parallels 230kV line 30 miles. Touch potential 25 VAC at valves. Station potentials: -850mV normal, -1,050mV near tower (200mV shift).
test_cases.task: Assess per NACE SP0177, determine touch voltage limits, mitigate interference.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 25 VAC EXCEEDS 15 VAC routine limit per SP0177. 200mV shift confirms interference. Grounding mats 6x6 feet required, zinc ribbon parallel, decoupling cells, annual surveys. Cost ~$75,000. SP0177 touch: routine 15 VAC, qualified 30 VAC.

test_cases.name: UT Shear Wave - Crack Sizing
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Circumferential crack per API 1104 Appendix E: 65-75% DAC, 6dB drop, sharp edges. ANY CRACK = AUTO-REJECT per Section 9.2.2. Removal 6 inches (1.25+2+2), suspect HIC due to hydrostatic context, metallurgical analysis needed.

test_cases.name: MFL Data Quality Assessment
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 3% poor characterization EXCEEDS 2% API 1163 limit. 5.0 ft/sec velocity exceeds recommended <4 ft/sec. Re-run 3.6 miles degraded data. 98% magnetization OK, no wheel slip. Manual verification option.

test_cases.name: Liquid Penetrant Testing Procedure
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: PT per ASTM E165: clean surface with solvent, apply penetrant 10-30 minutes dwell, remove excess (water washable or solvent), apply developer (dry or wet), wait 10 minutes, inspect under UV light (365 nm). Indications: linear = cracks, rounded = porosity. False positives: grinding marks. Temperature 50-125°F. Post-cleaning required. Limitations: surface breaking only, not for subsurface, rough surfaces problematic.

test_cases.name: Hardness Testing Conversion
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 5L X52: typically 150-190 HB (Brinell) or HRC 15-22. Rockwell B for softer materials. Conversion charts per ASTM E140. Field testing: Leeb rebound (HLD) converted to HRC/HB. Hardness limits: weld HAZ <250 HB typically. CVN correlation: higher hardness generally lower toughness. Automated conversion calculators available per API RP 5L3. Reproducibility ±5% for field methods.

test_cases.name: Magnetic Particle Yoke Calibration
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: ASME BPVC V: yoke verification with Pie gauge (ASTM E709) or quantifiable shims. Lift test: 10 lbs for AC, 40 lbs for DC yokes. Amperage: 1,000-1,500 amp-turns per inch of spacing. Technique: prod perpendicular to weld, overlap 15%, inspect in 45° increments. Demagnetization required if residual field >3 gauss. Wavelength: UV-A 365 nm for fluorescent. Wet vs dry method selection based on surface condition.

test_cases.name: Holiday Detection Threshold
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: NACE SP0274: detector calibrated on known holidays. FBE coating 16-40 mils: 67.5 volts (range 9-90 V per mil). Test voltage = coating thickness × 100 V typically. Sponge method for thin film. Brush spring pressure consistent. Inspection speed 12 inches/second max. Mark all holidays, repair per coating manufacturer, re-test 100%. Ground connection to bare metal. Weather: RH <85%, surface temp <120°F.

test_cases.name: Smart Pig Calibration Standards
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 1163 calibration: reference standards with known defects, pull-through verification run, speed calibration 1-10 ft/sec range, wall thickness verification at 5+ locations, odometer accuracy ±0.1%, clock synchronization, data format compliance PODS or similar, metal loss discrimination ±10%, axial location accuracy ±1%, circumferential ±5%. Field verification dig required if anomalies confirmed.

test_cases.name: Rebound Hammer Concrete Testing
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: ASTM C805: Schmidt hammer type N for 1-6 inch thickness. Calibration on anvil before/after. Grid pattern 12 readings average, discard outliers >15%. Correction factors: carbonation, surface condition, age, moisture. Angle correction if not perpendicular. Core correlation recommended for critical structures. Typical pipeline supports 3,000-5,000 psi compressive strength. Rebound values correlate to strength curves.

test_cases.name: Eddy Current Crack Detection
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: ET for heat exchanger tubes: absolute vs differential probe. Frequency selection based on material and wall (typically 1-100 kHz). Calibration with drilled holes and notches. Lift-off compensation for surface irregularities. Phase analysis for defect characterization. IACS conductivity scaling. Fill factor >80% for bobbin coils. Limitations: ferromagnetic materials problematic, limited penetration depth, requires calibration standards.

test_cases.name: Radiation Safety Officer Duties
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: NRC 10 CFR 34: RSO authorized user, specific training in radiation protection. Dose limits: 5 rem/year for whole body (general public 0.1 rem/year). ALARA principle: as low as reasonably achievable. Dosimetry: film badges for occupationally exposed, requiring TLDs (thermoluminescent) for extremities. Posting requirements: CAUTION for >2 mR/hr, HIGH RADIATION for >100 mR/hr. Security: 24-hour surveillance for Category sources. Lost source: notification within 30 days to NRC.

test_cases.name: TOFD Setup and Calibration
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Time-of-Flight Diffraction: two probes ( transmitter/receiver), parallel scan welded seam, lateral wave establishes time-zero, back wall reflection depth calibration. PCS (probe center spacing) calculation based on refracted angles. Frequency 2-10 MHz typical. A-scan, B-scan, D-scan displays. Defect sizing: depth from time-of-flight, length from scan distance. Critical for: lack of fusion detection, height sizing for fitness-for-service. Standards: ASTM E2373, BS 7706.

test_cases.name: Weld Heat Input Calculation
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Heat input (kJ/in) = (Voltage × Amperage × 60) / (Travel speed × 1,000). API 1104: maximum heat input 50 kJ/in for X52, 45 kJ/in for X65, 40 kJ/in for X70+. Excessive heat input: coarse grain, reduced toughness, distortion. Under-matching heat input: lack of fusion, incomplete penetration. WPS limits must be followed. Travel speed measured over 6-inch distance. Record: voltage, amperage, speed for each weld.

test_cases.name: Visual Weld Inspector Qualifications
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 1169 requirements: 3+ years experience, OR Level II MT/PT/UT certification, OR engineering degree with 1 year experience. Eye exam annually (Snellen 20/25 near and far, Jaeger #2). Color vision assessment for fluorescent inspection. Written practical examination by API. Recertification 3 years. AWS CWI also acceptable with NDT endorsements. Continuing education 15 hours per recertification period.

test_cases.name: Dimensional Tolerance Check
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 5L tolerances: wall thickness -12.5% to +15% on average, -10% at any point. OD ±1% for 2+", weight ±6.5%. Straightness 0.2% of length. End squareness 1.5 mm or 1.6 mm depending on OD. Pipe end bevel 30°+5°-0°. Length variation +50mm/-0mm for random. Measurement tools: ultrasonic thickness gauge (calibrated), pi tape for OD, laser straightness, visual squareness gauge.

test_cases.name: Phased Array Weld Scan Plan
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: PAUT per ASME V: sectorial scan angles 40-70° for butt welds. Number of elements 16-64. Focal laws calculated for beam coverage of full weld volume. Calibration on IIW block and AWS block for TC and amplitude. TCG (time-corrected gain) for depth compensation. Gate placement 0-200% of thickness. A-scan, S-scan, C-scan display modes. Encoder for mapping. DAC (distance amplitude correction). Procedure qualification essential variable compliance.

test_cases.name: Positive Material Identification
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: PMI methods: XRF handheld ( portable, quick), OES (accurate, larger sample), LIBS (carbon content capable). API 578 requires PMI for alloy materials at receipt, in-service, after repair. Testing points: random 10% minimum for critical service, 100% for high-temperature or sour service. Elements: Cr, Ni, Mo content verification against material cert. Documentation: heat number traceability. Resolution: 0.1% typically. Safety: laser eye protection, radiation awareness for XRF.

test_cases.name: Coating Mill Application QC
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: FBE process: surface prep Sa 2.5, anchor profile 2.0-4.0 mils, preheat 400-550°F, powder application 15-25 mils, gel time 8-12 seconds, cure schedule 3 minutes at 450°F. Holiday testing 67.5 V. Adhesion test: pull-off 500+ psi or Elcometer. Bend test 2.5°/D without disbondment. Cathodic disbondment: 1/8-inch max at 28 days. Impact: 80 in-lbs at 0°F. Mill certification review required.

test_cases.name: Pipe-to-Soil Potential Measurement
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: NACE SP0169: copper-copper sulfate reference electrode (CSE) standard. Measurement: digital voltmeter (high impedance 10 Mohm). Probe placement: 1-3 feet from pipe, in native soil not backfill. IR drop elimination: current interrupter, instant-off method. Potential criteria: -850 mV CSE (energized), -850 mV or more negative (instant-off), 100 mV polarization shift minimum. Test stations every mile maximum, more frequent in high consequence areas.

test_cases.name: Guided Wave Screening Distance
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: GWT setup: torsional or longitudinal modes, frequency selection 16-128 kHz typically. Range: 60-foot from transducer ring (coated), 30-foot uncoated, 120-foot in liquid-filled if coated. Resolution: 2-5% CSA detectable at 30 feet, 10% at 60 feet. Fitting flex indicator: bends, tees, supports create echoes. Screening identifies regions of interest, not flaw sizing. Dig verification required for anomalies. Reference standard for calibration. Temperature range -4°F to 140°F operation.

test_cases.name: In-Line Inspection Tool Selection
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: MFL for metal loss (general corrosion, pitting). UT for crack detection (axial, circumferential, SCC). Geometry for dents, ovality. EMAT for no-contact requirement (high temp). Caliper for internal geometry. Tool selection based on: threat identification, pipeline geometry, product, operating conditions. Speed requirement: 1-10 ft/sec. Passage feasibility: minimum radius check, valve bore check, launcher/receiver sizing. Data quality specification: depth accuracy ±10%, location ±1% length.

test_cases.name: SCC Detection Methods
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: High pH SCC: colony morphology, intergranular cracking. Near-neutral pH: transgranular, wide cracks. Detection: high-resolution MFL for clusters (not individual cracks), ultrasonic crack detection ( shear wave or phased array for axial cracks, circumferential for girth weld SCC). Hydrostatic testing: proof pressure to 1.5× MAOP for surviving cracks. SCCDA direct assessment per NACE SP0204. Crack sizing critical for FFS assessment per API 579. Excavation and coating removal for direct visual with MT/PT confirmation.

test_cases.name: Casing Assessment Requirements
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Casing pipe external corrosion: visual and UT for wall thickness. CP effectiveness: electrical isolation maintained (test station), insulator inspection annually. Shorts: metallic contact between carrier and casing. Flooding: casing vents inspection, end seals. Cathodic protection: casing not protectively coated, may require separate CP. Assessment intervals: external every 5 years, hydrostatic if internal corrosion suspected. Short creates shielding, not immediate hazard but reduces CP effectiveness on carrier. Repair: short removal, vent inspection, re-establish isolation.

test_cases.name: Flange Bolt Torque Application
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: ASME PCC-1 Appendix E: torque values based on gasket type (spiral wound vs RX vs IX), bolt material B7 vs B16 vs 660. Torque pattern: star pattern in three increments (30%, 60%, 100%). Torque wrench calibration within 1 year. Torque value = K×D×F (friction factor, diameter, preload). Torque-turn alternative: torque to snug then turn nut specified angle. Mark bolts after final torque. Re-torque after 24-hour settlement. Temperature limits for gasket relaxation.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Marker Inspection
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 192.707 requirements: markers every 800 feet in non-rural, every 300 feet in urban, at road crossings, at water crossings, buried test leads. Marker content: owner name, emergency number, product (gas pipeline), One-Call reference. Visibility: 4-foot vegetation clearance. Condition: legible, upright, not damaged. GPS coordinates in database. Annual patrol or ROW surveillance. Replacement if 25% illegible or knocked over. Additional markers if public activity near line.

test_cases.name: Above Ground Pipeline Support
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 570 inspection: support spacing per stress analysis, soil settlement at supports, corrosion under supports (CUPS inspection), pipe-to-support contact wear, thermal movement accommodation, expansion joint/loop inspection, weight distribution, load path changes, bolt tightness on supports, weld examination at shoe attachments. Concrete deterioration: spalling, rebar exposure. Sliding supports: maintenance of low-friction surface.

test_cases.name: Corrosion Coupon Installation
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: NACE SP0775: coupon types (C1018 steel for gas pipelines), flush vs extended mount, placement in flowstream not bypass, 6-month exposure minimum, triplicate coupons for statistical validity, orientation (horizontal for liquid, vertical for gas), metallurgical analysis for pitting. Calculation: mpy = (K×W)/(D×A×T) where K=constant, W=weight loss, D=density, A=area, T=time in years. Coupon retrieval without damage to sample.

test_cases.name: Bend Radius Verification
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 5L induction bends: minimum radius 5D for cold bends (field), 3D for hot (induction), 2.5D for thick-wall. Ovality limit 3% per 5L. Thinnest point measurement: wall reduction limit 12.5% of nominal. Wrinkle acceptance: none allowed for piggable lines. Ultrasonic thickness mapping: 8-point minimum per cross-section. Angle verification: ±2 degrees. Hardness: Hv 250 max for X65 to verify no damage.

test_cases.name: Pipeline ROW Encroachment
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Encroachment types: structures within easement, vegetation taller than 3 feet (visibility), excavation without notification, overhead power lines, storage materials. Actions: initial notification to landowner, legal action if not removed, compensation negotiation for permanent structures. Survey: GPS coordinates of encroachment, photo documentation. Clearances: 10 feet either side for maintenance access. No permanent structures within strip unless approved. Annual surveillance required.

test_cases.name: Weld Repair Procedure
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 1104 repair: excavation to visual 360° weld exposure, defect removal by grinding or air-arc, minimum removal 2 inches beyond visible defect, preheat per WPS (100°F minimum if ambient <50°F), repair weld sequence (opposite sides sequentially), examination 100% UT or RT. Repair limitations: one repair per defect location, if repair requires second removal then cut-out required, cut-out length 10 inches minimum, new weld full circumference.

test_cases.name: Odorant System Inspection
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 192.625 odorization: mercaptan or thiophane injection. Odorant level check weekly: tetrahedron threshold 1/5 or 5 odorant units. Equipment: pump or wick injection, flow-proportioning, tank level monitoring. Safety: PPE for handling (organic vapor cartridges), secondary containment, spill response kit. Injection rate 0.5-1.0 lb/MMscf typical, up to 2.0 for dense gas or odor fade. Inspection: leaks, injection pump operation, tank connections, vapor recovery.

test_cases.name: External Coating Inspection
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Holiday detection: 15-40 kV depending on coating thickness (100V/mil typical). Adhesion: peel test or pull-off per ASTM D4541. Thickness: magnetic gauge orToo measurement. Percent coverage: visual inspection for 100% coverage. RFI (random field inspection) if 95%+ coverage. Disbondment: holiday size measurement and mapping. Tape wrap: overlap minimum 1-inch, percentage of overlap per specification typically 55%. Shrink sleeves: patch size limits, overlap.

test_cases.name: Pressure Equipment Certification
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: ASME Section VIII vessel: nameplate data (MAWP, MDMT, year built, serial), inspection interval based on service (3-5 years typical), inspection scope includes thickness survey, internal/external examination, nozzle and shell welds (MT/PT), relief valve certification (stamped NB), inspection report per NBIC, R-stamp if repairs made, alteration documentation, pressure test if repairs, continued service if inspection acceptable. De-rating: documented engineering review, nameplate update.

test_cases.name: Valve Seat LeakTest Procedure
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 598 low pressure seat test: 80 psig air or gas test at both directions. Acceptable leakage: 0 bubbles for resilient seats (soft), 0 bubbles metal-to-metal. High pressure: 110% of rated pressure, duration 1× diameter minutes. Procedure: isolate valve, pressurize body cavity, check downstream for no flow. Bubble test: bubble counter in water. Alternative: pressure hold decay method. Soft seats: zero leakage acceptable. Metal seats: API allows minimal leakage if specified rate acceptable.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Right-of-Way Patrol
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Aerial patrol: 26 CFR 192.705 every 2 weeks in Class 3/4, monthly Class 1/2. Ground patrol: weekly in populated areas. Elements: vegetation clearing, excavation activity, erosion, exposed pipe, third-party activity, encroachments, access road condition, marker visibility. Documentation: GPS coordinate, photo, immediate hazards reported, 911 if emergency. Aircraft: visual above ground, 100-300 feet altitude, weather permitting. Helicopter: may hover for closer look.

test_cases.name: Compressor Valve Inspection
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Reciprocating compressor valves: poppet or plate type. Inspection intervals: run hours based on service (2,000-8,000 hours typical). Items: seat condition, spring strength (check vs new), wear on actuators, lifting mechanism, crack detection, carbon buildup, seat flatness. Testing: bench test before installation, leak test at 50 psig air. Reassembly: proper torque, orientation. Performance: reduced capacity, increased discharge temp indicate valve issues.

test_cases.name: Underwater Pipeline Survey
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Subsea inspection: ROV survey for exposed pipe, span measurement, anode depletion (electrical potential), macrofouling, coating damage. Span correction: grout bags, mechanical supports. Depth of burial verification. Cathodic protection: contact cell for potential measurement. Frequency: annual if exposed, less if buried. Water depth: diver inspection if shallow, ROV if deep. Acoustic: depth of cover sonar. Pipeline location: accurate to ±1 meter. Sediment changes: erosion or accumulation.

test_cases.name: Tank Floor Thickness Survey
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 653 tank floor: UT grid pattern typically 10-foot grid, additional points under shell. Minimum 5 readings per plate. Remaining life calculation: thickness data, corrosion rate, pit depth. Acceptable: above minimum structural per API 653 Table 4.2. Repair: patch plates if localized, complete floor replacement if widespread. Hydrostatic test after repairs. Product tightness: bottom tested if internal lining required. Shell plates also surveyed if suspected thinning.

test_cases.name: Welder Qualification Records
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 1104 welder qualification: valid 6 months minimum, must have continuity (weld within 6 months). Essential variables: position, progression, pipe diameter limits, wall thickness range, process (SMAW, GTAW, FCAW), filler metal, current/polarity. PQ test: guided bend (root and face), nick-break (fillet). Limits: diameter range qualified ±2× test diameter. Renewals: requalify if continuity lost. Record retention: 6 years minimum as-built. Visual acceptance: accept only undercuts, porosity per limits.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Composite Repair
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: ASME PCC-2 Article 4.1 composite sleeve: external corrosion repair, dents with <6% metal loss, gouges. Design: 4× defect length minimum sleeve, prep to near-white metal. Application: epoxy filler for profile, wrap with tension, overlap per procedure. Curing time temperature dependent. Qualification: 2-year testing per ISO TS 24817. Limitations: not for circumferential defects, not for cracks, not for internal corrosion. Inspection: holiday post-repair.

test_cases.name: Regulator Inspection Frequency
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: 192.740 pressure regulator inspection: each calendar year but not exceeding 15 months. Visual: external condition, setpoint pressure, response time, filter condition, relief valve operation. Testing: accuracy ±2% typical, inlet screen flow capacity. Slam-shut: trip on high/low test. Relief valve: lift setting verification, reseat tightness. Documentation: setpoint pressure, test results, maintenance performed. Replacement: if inaccurate or worn.

test_cases.name: Girth Weld Rejection Criteria
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: API 1104 automatic reject: any crack, any incomplete penetration, incomplete fusion >2 inches cumulative, undercut depth >1/32 inch or 12.5% wall, porosity exceeding limits, slag inclusions >2 inches cumulative. Linear indications: length, depth criteria by type. Repair: if repair requires then cut-out. Cut-out: remove defect plus 2 inches minimum each side, new weld full circumference. Documentation: NDE report showing defect location, size, type, disposition.

test_cases.name: Pipeline Test Lead Examination
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Test lead stations: wire bond to pipe, box for access, typically at mile markers, cased crossings, isolating joints, rectifier locations. Inspection: bond integrity, cable condition, box lid secure, accessibility clearing. Function: CP potential measurement, cathodic interference testing, coupon monitoring. Frequency: annual with CP survey. Repair: re-bond if resistance high, cable replacement if damaged. Documentation: station number, GPS, reading values.
